title: Zombie Rei revisited
wikitext:
[]: Zombie Rei revisited             Quote Post by eva_fan » Thu Nov 18, 2010 5:41 pm Hello folks. I could have used the welcome thread but the reason I joined up was to discuss so here's to discussion; I read the 'Zombie Rei' thread allready but nobody picked up on what I'm about to suggest, and the theory is slightly different from the discussion there. Also, as I'm brand new, I don't have the ability to make fancy screen caps to support my theories like OMF, Shin-seiki and the other protagonists do. This post won't be a masterpeice, please treat me gently. Zombie Rei, as seen near the very beggining where Shinji is using the phonebox is present throughout the rest of the story when characters die. Evidence; - Kaworu squished by Eva 01 - Misato after sending Shinji down the Elavator - Ritsuko gets shot Additionally the bridge characters (and obv. Gendo) see 'a' version of Rei during instrumentality, before the character morphs to whoever else. What about Toji? Is she around then? What about just before Shinji chokes Asuka at the end? There are screen caps of zombie Rei above the sea of LCL but I don't remember if this coincides with Shinji and Asuka. THEORY 1: Zombie Rei is in effect, the reaper. Death. This could be tied in with the links to Lillith and the 'watching over humanity' thing, and also the concept 'meeting your maker'. BUT. Right at the beggining, Shinji isn't dead. lets face it, he's pretty significant to the rest of the stroy. Unless; THEORY 2: Shinji DOES die. Most likely in the Angel attack. The rest of the story follows him through purgatory. Perhaps instrumentality represents peace in death? There are numerous tie-ins to support this notion (eg Gendo's wish to reunite his soul with Yui). Perhaps rejections of instrumentality keeps Shinji in purgatory, with the uncertainty of relationships, and other compromises as 'punishment'. I'd like to see what you think.

[]: Re: Zombie Rei revisited             Quote Post by Shinji » Thu Nov 18, 2010 11:03 pm Interesting, vedddy interesting. There's a few parts I find hard to swallow though. 1. When Kaworu was going squish squish in T.D. and Rei was present. At this point Rei is still very much alive, if only to the point of being a shiny new clone. Also, a huge A.T. field (bigger that Kaworu's) appears for a brief moment in Terminal Dogma around when Rei appears to us. So the suggestion that she is a, "zombeh," or otherwise wasn't actually there, is a moot point because she could've physically gone there herself. So why appear as an incorporeal ghost thing? 2. As extremely unlikely as this purgatory theory is, it's not impossible, but it also raises a lot more questions than it answers. One, why is Shinji in purgatory? Abandoned by his parents at a young age, forced to grow up on his own, and then he gets killed and must redeem himself in the afterlife? Sorry, doesn't make sense. If only for the reason that, if this was true, the story would focus a lot more on Shinji and spiritual crap, instead of SEELE and Gendo and the Evas and the Angels. 3. When Rei III becomes the all powerful GNR, she's described as having power equal to God. In essence, she becomes God, at least as far as anyone on Earth is concerned. It is strongly implied that GNR is able to transcend the restraints of time and therefore appear to people that are dying, even before she gains her powers. Ritsuko and Misato saw Rei because they were both on the verge of death. It is assumed/implied that she is kind of like a "soul collector" at this point. You can see her looking over all of the dead soldiers in the hallways. Clearly there's more of a point to this than, well nothing. However, when Instrumentality nears, GNR uses her omnipotent powers (all hail the giant naked lady, amen) to lower the A.T. fields or the emotional walls everyone has, by appearing to them and either embracing them, or by scaring the bejeezus out of them, or by just saying "watch this" and harvesting all the other souls on earth in an instant. There's one thing I'm at a loss to explain, and that is the appearance of Rei at the verry beginning in episode 1. Is GNR able to transcend all restraints of time? If so, did she appear to Toji? For that matter, did she appear to Ritsuko's mum? That'd be ironic. Did she appear to Asuka? I always assumed that she didn't if only for the fact that Asuka hates her. The most easily accepted explanation is that there is no explanation and that the appearance in the first episode is just a very very obscure form of foreshadowing that only makes sense after EoE. That in itself seems unlikely but it's the only thing I can come up with. So, in a way, yes you could say that Rei is like a grim reaper, a collector of souls. However, she is far from being "Death." In essence, she never kills anyone . Granted that when someone explodes into an orange liquid an their hand flies across the room and their spine is laying on the floor, it seems like WTF they just died?! But they really were only converted into LCL, the primordial ooze from whence they came. So, they're not actually dead, because it is implied that they have the ability to come back to life. Now, I'll TELL YOU WHO'S THE REAL ZOMBIE! Asuka! Why? She is the only one in the entire series who willfully brings herself literally back from the dead. She wasn't turned into LCL, she wasn't even alive during Instrumentality, she was very very dead. Now we get into a whole assumption that LCL is like the lifestream in FFVII, like when you die you go back there. This way Asuka was able to make herself independent again, but almost in the sense that she rejected the afterlife as well as intsrumentality. Tenacious little punk isn't she?[/i]

[]: Re: Zombie Rei revisited             Quote Post by esselfortium » Fri Nov 19, 2010 12:14 am Shinji wrote: Now, I'll TELL YOU WHO'S THE REAL ZOMBIE! Asuka! Why? She is the only one in the entire series who willfully brings herself literally back from the dead. She wasn't turned into LCL, she wasn't even alive during Instrumentality, she was very very dead. Now we get into a whole assumption that LCL is like the lifestream in FFVII, like when you die you go back there. This way Asuka was able to make herself independent again, but almost in the sense that she rejected the afterlife as well as intsrumentality. Tenacious little punk isn't she?

[]:             Quote Post by Shinji » Fri Nov 19, 2010 12:22 am I know I'm just sayin, hah But then, if that's true than could Yui come back? Or Kaji? Or Misato?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Fri Nov 19, 2010 12:50 am Yui remained inside Eva-01's core, of her own wacky volition, so no. For Kaji and Misato, I suppose it's up to interpretation about how long someone's physical body has to have been dead before their soul no longer is able to be picked up by Rei. Surely there has to be some sort of cutoff point, but we can only really guess at what it would be. Misato has much better chances than Kaji, though, I'd figure.

[]:             Quote Post by eva_fan » Fri Nov 19, 2010 5:43 am Hi Shinji, thanks for your input. Soul-collecting popped into my head after I made the post, I'll go along with that. Do we think all characters get a 'welcome home'? Re #1., 'regular' Rei was around during most of the other deaths too. Infact as a clear example of this, she was standing naked behind Gendo seconds before Ritsuko gets shot and flies past now-dressed and levitating 'zombie' Rei. I'm just referring to 'zombie' Rei in the manner of the other thread, in that it's the ghostly-aura version of Rei in a school uniform. This version of Rei also seems to hold two poses only, bolt upright almost with toes pointing straight down (like a ballerina), and standing over somebody with her head bowed. The first instance occurs at the beggining, and over the LCL (she is there, I watched again), the head-down pose over Misato, Ritsuko and the JSDF. Re #2., I fully accept that the main plot of the story is NOT to do with Shinji in purgatory. I don't think this discredits it, however. If zombie Rei is a grim or soul colector, then it's still a valid sub-plot. Do you have to 'deserve' purgatory? I make breif reference that the reason may be to force Shinji to choose between the happiness (and pain) brought upon by others and the no-pain no-gain loneliness. Maybe he even has to decide which one is heaven and which one is hell?

[]:             Quote Post by Shinji » Fri Nov 19, 2010 6:16 am eva_fan wrote: Do you have to 'deserve' purgatory? I make breif reference that the reason may be to force Shinji to choose between the happiness (and pain) brought upon by others and the no-pain no-gain loneliness. Maybe he even has to decide which one is heaven and which one is hell?

[]: Re: Zombie Rei revisited             Quote Post by esselfortium » Fri Nov 19, 2010 1:38 pm eva_fan wrote: THEORY 2: Shinji DOES die. Most likely in the Angel attack. The rest of the story follows him through purgatory. Perhaps instrumentality represents peace in death? There are numerous tie-ins to support this notion (eg Gendo's wish to reunite his soul with Yui). Perhaps rejections of instrumentality keeps Shinji in purgatory, with the uncertainty of relationships, and other compromises as 'punishment'. I'd like to see what you think.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Fri Nov 19, 2010 3:01 pm Shinji wrote: But maybe some evidence to the Purgatory theory is the fact that Shinji is one of the only characters, and quite possibly the only character that doesn't die and isn't shown being turned into LCL.

[]:             Quote Post by eva_fan » Fri Nov 19, 2010 4:24 pm esselfortium, perhaps not. I'm not certain on what I beleive the choice between instrumentality and seperate entity life to represent in the purgatory scenario. What I've put forward so far are only musings. I would argue that Shinji did get tanged, when Rei is sitting on/in him explaining they are in the LCL primordial soup I don't know how else he could be there without that happening. Does anyone have an answer for if zombie Rei is around at Toji's death yet? I don't have the series to hand, only the movies.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Nov 19, 2010 5:09 pm esselfortium wrote: Yui remained inside Eva-01's core, of her own wacky volition, so no. For Kaji and Misato, I suppose it's up to interpretation about how long someone's physical body has to have been dead before their soul no longer is able to be picked up by Rei. Surely there has to be some sort of cutoff point, but we can only really guess at what it would be. Misato has much better chances than Kaji, though, I'd figure.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinji » Fri Nov 19, 2010 7:01 pm BiQ wrote: Considering all the other crazy shit that goes around in EoE, I don't find it hard at all to believe Shinji can get tanged within Eva 01 while the Eva itself stays just fine.... Not to mention that tree of life thingy that engulfs/absorbs/is made from Eva 01 immediately [s]after[/s]before those spots I posted, when Lance of Longinus fuses with it.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Nov 20, 2010 4:07 am Shinji wrote: a while since I've seen the first movie tho so if he died in that then i have no idea.

Article: Zombie Rei revisited
.T. fields or the emotional walls everyone has, by appearing to them and either embracing them, or by scaring the bejeezus out of them, or by just saying "watch this" and harvesting all the other souls on earth in an instant. There's one thing I'm at a loss to explain, and that is the appearance of Rei at the verry beginning in episode 1. Is GNR able to transcend all restraints of time? If so, did she appear to Toji? For that matter, did she appear to Ritsuko's mum? That'd be ironic. Did she appear to Asuka? I always assumed that she didn't if only for the fact that Asuka hates her. The most easily accepted explanation is that there is no explanation and that the appearance in the first episode is just a very very obscure form of foreshadowing that only makes sense after EoE. That in itself seems unlikely but it's the only thing I can come up with. So, in a way, yes you could say that Rei is like a grim reaper, a collector of souls. However, she is far from being "Death." In essence, she never kills anyone . Granted that when someone explodes into an orange liquid an their hand flies across the room and their spine is laying on the floor, it seems like WTF they just died?! But they really were only converted into LCL, the primordial ooze from whence they came. So, they're not actually dead, because it is implied that they have the ability to come back to life. Now, I'll TELL YOU WHO'S THE REAL ZOMBIE! Asuka! Why? She is the only one in the entire series who willfully brings herself literally back from the dead. She wasn't turned into LCL, she wasn't even alive during Instrumentality, she was very very dead. Now we get into a whole assumption that LCL is like the lifestream in FFVII, like when you die you go back there. This way Asuka was able to make herself independent again, but almost in the sense that she rejected the afterlife as well as intsrumentality. Tenacious little punk isn't she?[/i]

For Kaji and Misato, I suppose it's up to interpretation about how long someone's physical body has to have been dead before their soul no longer is able to be picked up by Rei. Surely there has to be some sort of cutoff point, but we can only really guess at what it would be. Misato has much better chances than Kaji, though, I'd figure.
